skill.name: agent-dev
skill.description: Create, configure, and update agents — including their prompt, model, and skill / MCP dependencies. Use when the user asks to build, configure, modify, or debug an agent.

skill.name: airtable
skill.description: Read and write Airtable bases via the REST API — list and filter records, create/update/delete rows, run upsert operations, and query views. Use when an agent needs to query, mutate, or sync data stored in an Airtable base.

requiredCredentials.name: AIRTABLE_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Airtable personal access token from https://airtable.com/create/tokens

requiredCredentials.name: AIRTABLE_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Airtable personal access token from https://airtable.com/create/tokens

skill.name: architecture-diagram
skill.description: Create professional, dark-themed architecture diagrams as standalone HTML files with SVG graphics. Use when the user asks for system architecture diagrams, infrastructure diagrams, cloud architecture visualizations, security diagrams, network topology diagrams, or any technical diagram showing system components and their relationships.

skill.name: ascii-art
skill.description: Generate ASCII art — text banners (pyfiglet), styled boxes and quotes (cowsay, boxes), and image-to-ASCII conversion. Use for terminal-friendly logos, README headers, chat decorations, or anywhere monospaced visual output is needed.

skill.name: ascii-video
skill.description: Convert video and audio files into colored ASCII MP4s or GIFs using ffmpeg + Python. Optional ElevenLabs TTS integration for voiced narration over the rendered output.

skill.name: audio-generation
skill.description: Convert text to speech and transcribe audio using AI models (OpenAI TTS, ElevenLabs, Whisper). Use when users want to generate audio from text or transcribe speech to text.

skill.name: baoyu-comic
skill.description: Generate knowledge comics — multi-panel illustrated explainers for educational topics, biographies, or step-by-step tutorials. Produces image prompts, generates panels, and assembles the final comic.

skill.name: baoyu-infographic
skill.description: Design infographics by combining one of 21 layouts with one of 21 visual styles. Useful for visualizing data, processes, comparisons, or summaries as a single high-quality image.

skill.name: caveman
skill.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says caveman mode, talk like caveman, use caveman, less tokens, be brief, or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.

skill.name: codebase-inspection
skill.description: Inspect a codebase to report total lines of code, language breakdown, and code/comment/blank ratios using pygount. Use when sizing a repository, comparing language distribution, or producing engineering metrics for a report.

skill.name: comfyui
skill.description: Generate images, video, and audio with ComfyUI on Comfy Cloud — submit workflows, inject parameters, monitor jobs, and download outputs via the hosted REST/WebSocket API.

requiredCredentials.name: COMFY_CLOUD_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Comfy Cloud API key from https://platform.comfy.org/login (used as X-API-Key header for hosted ComfyUI)

requiredCredentials.name: COMFY_CLOUD_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Comfy Cloud API key from https://platform.comfy.org/login (used as X-API-Key header for hosted ComfyUI)

skill.name: concept-diagrams
skill.description: Produce flat, minimal SVG diagrams in light and dark modes for explaining ideas. Ideal for educational content, slide visuals, or documentation. Output is a single self-contained HTML file.

skill.name: design-md
skill.description: Author, validate, and export DESIGN.md token spec files in Google's design.md format. Use when defining design tokens (colors, typography, spacing) for a product, or when checking a token spec against the built-in lint rules including WCAG contrast.

skill.name: docx
skill.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.

skill.name: duckduckgo-search
skill.description: Search the web for free via DuckDuckGo — supports text, news, image, and video search with no API key required. Useful as a fallback when a managed web-search primitive is not available.

skill.name: excalidraw
skill.description: Generate hand-drawn-style Excalidraw diagrams as JSON files — architecture, flow, sequence, and freeform sketches. Output can be opened directly in Excalidraw or exported to images.

skill.name: fitness-nutrition
skill.description: Plan gym workouts and track nutrition. Pulls 690+ exercises with form cues from the wger database and 380,000+ foods with macros/micros from the USDA FoodData Central API. Use when designing training programs, looking up exercise technique, or logging meals.

skill.name: gif-search
skill.description: Search Tenor for GIFs by keyword and download the best match. Use when an agent needs to fetch a relevant animated GIF for a chat reply, social post, or document.

requiredCredentials.name: TENOR_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Tenor API key from https://developers.google.com/tenor/guides/quickstart

requiredCredentials.name: TENOR_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Tenor API key from https://developers.google.com/tenor/guides/quickstart

skill.name: github-auth
skill.description: Set up GitHub authentication: HTTPS personal access tokens, SSH keys, and `gh` CLI login. Use as a prerequisite for any other GitHub workflow when the user is not yet authenticated on the host.

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token (classic or fine-grained) used by the gh CLI and git over HTTPS

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token (classic or fine-grained) used by the gh CLI and git over HTTPS

skill.name: github-code-review
skill.description: Review GitHub pull requests — read diffs, leave inline comments, approve or request changes. Works via the `gh` CLI or the REST API.

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with repo and pull-request scopes

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with repo and pull-request scopes

skill.name: github-issues
skill.description: Create, triage, label, assign, and close GitHub issues via the `gh` CLI or the REST API. Use for bug intake, backlog grooming, or automating issue workflows.

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with issues:write scope

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with issues:write scope

skill.name: github-pr-workflow
skill.description: Manage the GitHub pull request lifecycle — create branches, commit (Conventional Commits enforced), open PRs, monitor CI status, and merge.

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with repo and workflow scopes

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with repo and workflow scopes

skill.name: github-repo-management
skill.description: Clone, create, fork, and configure GitHub repositories; manage remotes and cut releases.

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with repo and admin:org scopes

requiredCredentials.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: GitHub personal access token with repo and admin:org scopes

skill.name: google-workspace
skill.description: Read and write across Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, and Sheets via the Google Python client. Auth is delegated to the platform vault — the skill reads a vault-issued OAuth access token from $MCP_GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_ACCESS_TOKEN.

skill.name: humanizer
skill.description: Rewrite AI-generated text to sound human — strips 29 common AI tells (em-dashes, 'in conclusion', tricolons, hedging) and adds natural cadence while preserving meaning.

skill.name: image-generation
skill.description: Generate, edit, and create variations of images using AI models (GPT-Image, DALL-E, Gemini). Use when users want to create new images from descriptions, edit existing images, or create variations.

skill.name: manim-video
skill.description: Produce 3Blue1Brown-style animated math and algorithm explainer videos using Manim Community Edition. Heavy dependencies (manim, full LaTeX, ffmpeg).

skill.name: maps
skill.description: Geocode addresses, find points of interest by category, compute driving/walking/cycling routes, and look up timezones using OpenStreetMap and OSRM. No API key required.

skill.name: meme-generation
skill.description: Generate real meme images by selecting a template from imgflip and overlaying caption text with Pillow.

skill.name: nano-pdf
skill.description: Edit PDF text in place using the nano-pdf CLI with natural-language prompts — fix typos, change titles, update small sections without re-typesetting the document.

skill.name: ocr-and-documents
skill.description: Extract text from PDFs and scanned documents using pymupdf for native PDFs and marker-pdf for high-quality OCR on scans. Marker-pdf is heavy (~5GB models, optional GPU acceleration).

skill.name: p5js
skill.description: Generate p5.js sketches as single self-contained HTML files — generative art, shaders, interactive scenes, and 3D. Optional Puppeteer + ffmpeg pipeline to capture sketches as MP4 or GIF.

skill.name: parallel-cli
skill.description: Run web search, structured extraction, deep research, and dataset enrichment via the Parallel API. Paid third-party service.

requiredCredentials.name: PARALLEL_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Parallel.ai API key from https://parallel.ai (or run `parallel-cli login`)

requiredCredentials.name: PARALLEL_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Parallel.ai API key from https://parallel.ai (or run `parallel-cli login`)

skill.name: pdf
skill.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.

skill.name: pixel-art
skill.description: Generate pixel art with era-appropriate palettes — NES, Game Boy, PICO-8, and other retro consoles. Supports stills and short animated sequences.

skill.name: polymarket
skill.description: Query Polymarket prediction markets — list markets, fetch prices, read orderbooks, and pull historical data. Read-only; no trade execution.

skill.name: popular-web-designs
skill.description: Reference HTML/CSS templates of 54 real design systems (Stripe, Linear, Vercel, etc.). Use when designing a marketing page or component in the visual style of a known brand.

skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.

skill.name: project-dev
skill.description: Create, configure, and manage projects within a pod — including attaching and removing agents. Use when the user wants to set up a project, add or change agents in a project, or configure project settings.

skill.name: onboarding
skill.description: First-session setup flow for a new Ren user. Triggered when the user's first message starts with "Onboard me to Ren". Isolates one concrete workflow, decides MCPs and skills with the user, then scaffolds one agent inside a NEW project in the user's current pod. Do not invoke for any other purpose.

skill.name: remotion-best-practices
skill.description: Best practices for building videos with Remotion (React-based video framework). Covers new project setup, compositions, sequencing, audio/video, text animations, GIFs, transitions, timing, Google Fonts, local fonts, Lottie, 3D, subtitles, and FFmpeg usage. Use whenever working on Remotion code or scaffolding a new video project.

skill.name: scrapling
skill.description: Scrape the web — HTTP fetching, stealth browser automation, Cloudflare bypass, and full-site spidering. Requires post-install browser-binary setup.

skill.name: sherlock
skill.description: Hunt for accounts of a given username across 400+ social media and online platforms (OSINT). Verify the user has authorization before running.

skill.name: shopify
skill.description: Shopify Admin & Storefront GraphQL APIs via curl. Manage products, orders, customers, inventory, metafields, bulk operations, and webhooks. Use when working with a Shopify store programmatically — listing products, updating inventory, pulling orders, managing customers, or setting metafields.

requiredCredentials.name: SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: Shopify Admin API access token (starts with shpat_) from your custom app in Settings -> Apps and sales channels -> Develop apps

requiredCredentials.name: SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN
requiredCredentials.description: Permanent myshopify.com subdomain, e.g. my-store.myshopify.com (not the custom domain)

requiredCredentials.name: SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN
requiredCredentials.description: Shopify Admin API access token (starts with shpat_) from your custom app in Settings -> Apps and sales channels -> Develop apps

requiredCredentials.name: SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN
requiredCredentials.description: Permanent myshopify.com subdomain, e.g. my-store.myshopify.com (not the custom domain)

skill.name: skill-dev
skill.description: Author and edit custom skills (the prompt-shaped instructions agents follow). Use when the user asks to create, update, modify, or optimize a skill, or wants to add a custom capability to an agent.

skill.name: soultrace
skill.description: Take a personality assessment via the SoulTrace API. Use when the user wants to take a personality test, discover their psychological archetype, understand their personality traits, or get a color-based personality profile. The API uses a 5-color psychological model (White=structure, Blue=understanding, Black=agency, Red=intensity, Green=connection) with Bayesian adaptive question selection to classify users into one of 25 archetypes. Triggers on: personality test, personality assessment, what's my personality, take a quiz, archetype test, color personality, soultrace.

skill.name: typefully
skill.description: Create, schedule, and manage social media posts via Typefully — X, LinkedIn, Threads, Bluesky, Mastodon. Drafts, scheduling, queue, comments, analytics, and media upload. Use when asked to draft, schedule, post, or check social posts.

requiredCredentials.name: TYPEFULLY_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Typefully API key from https://typefully.com/?settings=api

requiredCredentials.name: TYPEFULLY_API_KEY
requiredCredentials.description: Typefully API key from https://typefully.com/?settings=api

skill.name: video-generation
skill.description: Generate videos from text prompts using AI models (Sora, Veo). Use when users want to create videos from descriptions, check generation status, download completed videos, or list generated videos.

skill.name: xlsx
skill.description: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.

skill.name: xurl
skill.description: Interact with X (Twitter) via the xurl CLI — post tweets, search, send DMs, upload media, and call any v2 API endpoint. The xurl tool manages OAuth credentials automatically in ~/.xurl after one-time login.

skill.name: youtube-content
skill.description: Pull a YouTube transcript and turn it into summaries, Twitter/X threads, or blog posts. Use when an agent needs to extract or repurpose spoken content from a YouTube video.

skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to review my UI, check accessibility, audit design, review UX, or check my site against best practices.
